t: Sam says 1/4 is bigger than 1/2 because 4 is bigger than 2. What should you tell Sam?
o: More parts means smaller pieces, so 1/4 is actually smaller
e: Sam is wrong. Cutting into MORE pieces makes each piece SMALLER. 1/4 < 1/2.

t: Ella ate 3/8 of a cake. Her brother ate 4/8. How much did they eat together? Is more than half the cake gone?
o: 7/8 -- yes, more than half is gone
7/8 -- no, less than half is gone
e: 3/8 + 4/8 = 7/8. Half is 4/8. Since 7/8 > 4/8, yes, way more than half is gone!

t: You have 7 fourths of a pie. How many whole pies is that, and how much extra?
e: 4/4 = 1 whole. 7/4 = 4/4 + 3/4 = 1 whole pie and 3/4 extra.

t: A rectangle is cut into 8 parts. 5 parts are wide and 3 parts are narrow. Is 3/8 shaded if the 3 narrow parts are colored?
o: Yes, because 3 out of 8 are colored
No, because the parts are not all equal
No, because only wide parts count
e: The parts are not equal (5 wide, 3 narrow). Fractions need equal parts, so we cannot say 3/8.

t: 4 kids share 3 pies equally. What fraction of a pie does each kid get?
e: 3 pies split among 4 kids = 3/4 per kid. Each child gets 3 fourths of one pie.

t: Order these fractions from smallest to largest: 3/4, 3/8, 1/2.
Kai ate 2/4 of a pizza. Zoe ate 3/8 of the same size pizza. Who ate more?
e: 2/4 = 4/8. Compare 4/8 to 3/8. Since 4 > 3, Kai ate more.

t: Mia says 6/8 and 3/4 are different amounts because the numbers are different. Is she right?
o: Yes, different numbers mean different amounts
e: Mia is wrong. 3/4 = 6/8 because each fourth splits into 2 eighths. They show the same amount.

t: A pie is cut into 8 equal slices. Dad eats 1/4 of the pie and Mom eats 1/2 of the pie. How many slices are left?
e: Dad eats 1/4 = 2/8 (2 slices). Mom eats 1/2 = 4/8 (4 slices). 2 + 4 = 6 eaten. 8 - 6 = 2 slices left.

t: Ben has 5/8 of a candy bar. He says that is more than 1/2 but less than 1 whole. Is he correct?
e: 1/2 = 4/8 and 1 whole = 8/8. Since 4/8 < 5/8 < 8/8, Ben is correct!

t: Leo says 2/8 is bigger than 1/4 because 2 > 1 and 8 > 4. Is Leo right?
e: Leo is wrong! 1/4 = 2/8. They are the same amount. You cannot just compare top and bottom separately.

t: 3 friends share 2 cakes equally. What fraction of a cake does each friend get?
e: 2 cakes shared among 3 friends = 2/3 per friend. Each gets 2 thirds of a cake.

t: Ava colors 1/2 of a circle red, then 1/4 blue, then 1/8 green. What fraction is still white?
Nora has 3 halves of a granola bar. Eli has 5 fourths of a granola bar. Who has more?
o: You cannot compare halves and fourths
e: 3/2 = 6/4. Compare 6/4 to 5/4. Since 6 > 5, Nora has more.

t: A class votes for snacks. 3/8 want chips, 2/8 want fruit, and the rest want pretzels. What fraction wants pretzels? Which snack is most popular?
o: 3/8 pretzels -- pretzels tie with chips for most popular
e: 3/8 + 2/8 = 5/8 accounted for. 8/8 - 5/8 = 3/8 want pretzels. Chips and pretzels tie at 3/8 each.

t: Jada says if you know 1/4 < 1/2, then you also know 3/4 < 3/2. Is she right?
e: Jada is correct! 3/4 = 0.75 and 3/2 = 1.5. Fourths are smaller pieces than halves, so 3 fourths < 3 halves.

t: A garden has 8 equal sections. Tomatoes take 3/8, carrots take 1/4, and flowers take 1/8. What fraction is empty?
e: In eighths: tomatoes 3/8, carrots 1/4 = 2/8, flowers 1/8. Total planted: 3 + 2 + 1 = 6/8. Empty: 8/8 - 6/8 = 2/8.

t: Which is closest to 1 whole without going over: 3/4, 7/8, or 5/8?
o: They are all the same distance
e: In eighths: 3/4 = 6/8, 5/8 = 5/8, 7/8 = 7/8. 7/8 is only 1/8 away from 8/8. That is the closest.

t: Ravi and Mia each have a same-size sandwich. Ravi eats 5/8 of his. Mia eats 3/4 of hers. Who ate more? How much more (in eighths)?
e: 3/4 = 6/8. Compare 5/8 and 6/8. Mia ate 6/8 - 5/8 = 1/8 more than Ravi.

t: Ian says 4/8, 2/4, and 1/2 should NOT all be at the same spot on a number line. Is Ian right?
o: Yes, they have different numbers so different spots
No, they are all equal and belong at the same spot
No, but only 2/4 and 1/2 are at the same spot
e: Ian is wrong. 4/8 = 2/4 = 1/2. They all equal the same amount and sit at the same point on the number line.
